questions.text: A computer consists of both hardware and software.

subparts.text: There are two types of software. Identify the types.

questions.text: Tick (✓) whether the following statements refer to a Command Line Interface (CLI) or a Graphical User Interface (GUI).

rows: The user has to type in every instruction
The user does not need to learn any of the instructions
Each instruction has to be typed in correctly
The user is in direct communication with the computer

questions.text: Every computer contains input and output devices and a central processing unit (CPU).

subparts.text: Describe the CPU including its role.

Data can be read directly by using a number of different devices. Name the most appropriate device used to read the following data from a cheque: Cheque No., Branch Code, Account No., Transaction Code.

questions.text: A hospital is creating a database to store blood donors' medical details. The fields in the database include Health_number, Date_of_birth, Gender and Blood_group.

subparts.text: When a new blood donor wishes to give blood they need to fill in a form. Each blood donor has a unique health number which uses a 10-digit number, for example 9434765919. Blood group can be A, B, AB or O. Name a suitable data type for the answers given to each of the questions in the form. For any numeric field, specify the type of number.
In the Blood_group field the only data that can be entered is A, B, AB or O. Tick (✓) whether each of the following blood groups is an example of abnormal or normal data.

The administrator needs a list of all the blood donors in the database with blood group A as well as in the same list all the blood donors with blood group AB. This list will only include female blood donors. Write a query to find all the female blood group donors with the blood group A as well as those with AB.
When the details of a new donor are added to the database the person's date of birth is entered. Explain why the administrator would prefer their date of birth to be stored rather than their age.
Name the most appropriate validation check for the Health_number.

questions.text: Phishing and pharming can affect the security of a user's data.

subparts.text: Compare and contrast phishing and pharming.
Smishing is another security issue. Describe the methods which can be used to help prevent smishing.

questions.text: Tick (✓) which of the following statements apply to LANs, which apply to WANs and which apply to WLANs.

rows: The internet is an example of this type of network
This type of network is found in a building but is connected without cables
This type of network is used to transmit data between Europe and North America
This type of network is found in a building but is connected with cables

questions.text: The head teacher of a school has decided to replace desktop computers with laptop computers. Describe the advantages and disadvantages of using a laptop computer rather than a desktop computer.
Following recent data protection issues the personal data we allow organisations to share about us may be reduced.

subparts.text: Discuss the benefits and drawbacks of trying to keep personal data confidential and secure.
The sharing of email addresses can lead to spam. Explain what is meant by the term spam email.
Explain why spam email should be prevented.

questions.text: Point of sale (POS) terminals are used in supermarkets.

subparts.text: Tick (✓) which of the following devices used at a POS terminal are only input devices, which are only output devices and which are both.

rows: Light sensor on the conveyor belt

subparts.text: A stock file contains data about the goods being sold. Describe the steps needed to update the stock file when an item is scanned at the point of sale (POS) terminal. Include in your description how new stock can be ordered automatically.

questions.text: The network manager at a school has been asked to develop a student registration system to help staff keep a record of student attendance.

subparts.text: Describe the steps required in the analysis of such a system.

The school is planning to use fingerprints as a way to record when students arrive in the class. Describe the advantages and disadvantages of using fingerprint data entry for student registration systems rather than magnetic ID cards.
Fingerprints are one example of biometrics. Identify three other examples of biometrics.

questions.text: Most data protection acts include the principle that data should be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date. List four other principles of a typical data protection act.

The way we buy products using credit cards has changed in recent years. Originally the magnetic stripe on the back of the card was used with a signature. Then chip and PIN methods were used, and more recently contactless methods have been introduced. Discuss the effectiveness of using these different methods to improve security when paying for goods.
